labels.name: duplicate

labels.name: wontfix
labels.description: This will not be worked on

issues.title: Attachment field should be `purpose`, not `type`, to match vcon-core-02
issues.author: howethomas
issues.body:
## Summary

The draft (`-01`) specifies that the lawful basis attachment uses a top-level `type: "lawful_basis"` field, but `draft-ietf-vcon-vcon-core-02` defines the attachment-purpose field as `purpose`, not `type`. There is no `type` parameter on the core Attachment Object.

## Where in the draft

- §Attachment Container: *"type: MUST be set to 'lawful_basis'"*
- §Example Lawful Basis Attachment: `"type": "lawful_basis"` (~line 323)
- §Extension Usage example: `"type": "lawful_basis"` (~line 240)
- IANA §Attachment Object Parameter Names Registry requests registration of `type` and notes: *"This addresses the 'TODO: type or purpose' noted in Section 6.3.6 of [I-D.draft-ietf-vcon-vcon-core]."*

## Why it's a problem

vcon-core has since resolved that TODO. `draft-ietf-vcon-vcon-core-02` §Attachment Object defines only `purpose` (see §attachment-purpose); it does not define `type`. Implementations following core will look at `purpose` and skip lawful-basis attachments emitted per this draft. The `vcon-dev/vcon-speckit` and `VCON_USAGE_GUIDE.md` examples already use `purpose: "lawful_basis"`.

## Suggested fix

1. Change `type` → `purpose` throughout §Attachment Container, §Extension Usage, and the example block.
2. Drop the IANA registration of `type` as a new Attachment Object parameter name.
3. Optionally add an interop note that consumers MAY also accept legacy `type: "lawful_basis"`.

issues.title: Example `body` is shown as inline JSON object but `encoding: "json"` requires a string
issues.author: howethomas
issues.body:
## Summary

§Example Lawful Basis Attachment shows `body` as an inline JSON object, but the same example sets `encoding: "json"`. Per `draft-ietf-vcon-vcon-core-02` §Attachment Object and the Inline Files convention, attachment `body` is a string and `encoding: "json"` means the string is JSON-encoded — not that `body` is a parsed JSON value.

## Where in the draft

- §Extension Usage example (~line 245): `"body": { ... }` with `"encoding": "json"`
- §Example Lawful Basis Attachment (~line 328): same pattern
- §Lawful Basis Body Structure: *"body: MUST contain the lawful basis data structure as defined below"* — wording is ambiguous between "the string contains the encoded structure" and "body is the structure."

## Why it's a problem

Spec-strict consumers will fail to decode the body (type mismatch: dict vs. string). Healthcare and contact-center examples in `vcon-dev/vcon-speckit/VCON_USAGE_GUIDE.md` correctly emit `body` as a JSON-encoded string (e.g. `"body": "{\"lawful_basis\": ...}"`), so the draft's example is the outlier.

## Suggested fix

1. Update the two examples so `body` is a JSON-encoded string with proper escaping (or a multi-line string with appropriate JSON formatting).
2. Clarify §Lawful Basis Body Structure: *"The `body` field MUST be a string containing a JSON-encoded object with the following structure, paired with `encoding: 'json'`."*
3. Optionally add a non-normative note showing the decoded structure separately for readability.

issues.title: `party` and `dialog` should be REQUIRED, not SHOULD, to align with vcon-core-02
issues.author: howethomas
issues.body:
## Summary

§Attachment Container lists `party` and `dialog` as SHOULD-include fields. `draft-ietf-vcon-vcon-core-02` makes both REQUIRED on every Attachment Object. The lawful-basis draft is laxer than core, which leaves a strict-core implementation in an awkward position when consuming attachments emitted per this draft.

## Where in the draft

§Attachment Container:
> *"The lawful basis attachment SHOULD include: start, party, dialog"*

## Why it's a problem

A strict vcon-core consumer will reject attachments missing `party` or `dialog`. Implementations following this draft's permissive language can produce non-portable artifacts. The conventional vCon-level placement (`party: 0, dialog: 0`) costs nothing to require and matches the speckit's guidance.

## Suggested fix

1. Promote `party` and `dialog` from SHOULD to MUST.
2. Add a sentence: *"For lawful-basis records that apply to the entire vCon rather than a specific dialog or party, set `party: 0` and `dialog: 0`."*
3. Keep `start` as SHOULD (it's not required by core).
